fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer pharmacist license to hawaii, pharmacist compact hawaii, hawaii pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Hawaii Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance
board.mailing_address:
Board of Pharmacy
DCCA, PVL Licensing Branch
P.O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Hawaii.

steps.title: Create MyPVL Account and Apply
steps.description: Register for an account on the MyPVL system (https://pvl.ehawaii.gov/mypvl/) and submit the 'Pharmacist by Endorsement' application, paying the $50 initial application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official pharmacy school transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited pharmacy institution to the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Request NABP Score Transfer
steps.description: Request the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to transfer your official NAPLEX and MPJE scores directly to the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licensure
steps.description: Complete the 'Verification of Licensure' form for all states where you currently hold or have held a pharmacist license, ensuring the respective boards send it directly to the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Application & Await Review
steps.description: Upload any remaining required documents to your MyPVL application and await the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy's review and approval. Upon approval, an additional $198 license fee will be required.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Hawaii
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Hawaii, applicants must submit their application through the MyPVL online system managed by the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA) Professional and Vocational Licensing Division.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MyPVL portal, click 'Register' to create a new account, and follow the prompts to set up your username and password before logging in to start an application.
